message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_logcumsumexp autograd via apply_function with reverse-cumsum backward [bd-1tax]

Last child of frankentorch-1tax (severed-autograd statistical /
special ops umbrella). Previously tensor_logcumsumexp had a
fail-loud guard rejecting tracked inputs because the body extracted
storage, computed the stable forward, and rebuilt a
requires_grad=false leaf. Same severed-autograd pattern recently
fixed for gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma +
multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6).

Wrap the stable forward in tensor_apply_function with the
analytical backward. For y[i] = log(sum_{j<=i} exp(x[j])),
    dy[i]/dx[k] = exp(x[k] - y[i])  if k <= i, else 0
so the vector-Jacobian product is
    dL/dx[k] = sum_{i>=k} dL/dy[i] * exp(x[k] - y[i])
which is bounded since x[k] <= y[i] for i >= k. Both x and y are
saved for backward; the O(D^2)-per-slice direct sum avoids
precision pitfalls of an explicit reverse cumsum of
grad_y[i] * exp(-y[i]) when y[i] is large.

Adds two regression tests:
  * logcumsumexp_propagates_gradient_via_reverse_cumsum: x=[1,2,3]
    under sum loss, asserts grad against the analytical
    grad[k] = e^{x_k} * sum_{i>=k} 1/S_i with 1e-12 tolerance.
  * logcumsumexp_gradient_stable_for_large_values: x=[100,100,100]
    where exp(-y[i]) underflows. By symmetry grad collapses to the
    harmonic-style sum [11/6, 5/6, 1/3] and stays finite — the
    direct (xk - yi).exp() form keeps every term in [0, 1].

ft-api lib tests: 1135 passing (+2 from 1133).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
